articles.title: Centre, Delhi govt launch major road projects for future-ready NCR
articles.description: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta announced a series of large-scale road projects jointly undertaken by the Centre and Delhi Government. The projects aim to ease traffic congestion and modernise the transport network in the Delhi-NCR region with new expressways.

articles.title: PMC sets June 15 deadline for pipeline works
articles.description: Patna Municipal Corporation targets June 15 deadline to finish 26 water pipeline projects and road restoration before monsoon; 350 of 760 works completed.

articles.title: Government looks at involving Air Force for safe transport of NEET papers
articles.description: It is not immediately known whether the IAF will provide logistics support for any other all-India level examination as well.

articles.title: RPF cracks down on illegal railway e-ticket booking
articles.description: Railway Protection Force (RPF) of South Western Railways has cracked down on illegal railway e-ticket booking activities in Mysuru and Hassan.

articles.title: EU import cuts threaten Ukraine's battered steel sector, Interpipe CEO says
articles.description: Ukraine's vital steel sector faces a new threat from European Union import quotas. This move could severely damage the war-torn industry, despite prior EU assurances. Ukrainian steel producers are already struggling with war-related challenges. The EU's decision risks significant economic losses for Ukraine and contradicts its stated support for the nation. This impacts revenue and tax collection.

articles.title: Bengaluru founder quit ₹75 LPA job, went broke a year later but says he has ‘zero regrets’
articles.description: A Bengaluru startup founder left a ₹75 lakh per annum corporate job to build his own AI company, Eklyai. Despite facing financial struggles, he believes the decision was the best, valuing the freedom, constant learning, and sense of purpose gained over corporate politics and performative culture.

articles.title: Air Cargo demand up 4.0% in April amid West Asia disruption: IATA
articles.description: Asia-Pacific airlines saw a 10.5% year-on-year growth in air cargo demand in April, the strongest rise of all regions. Capacity increased by 5.3% year-on-year.

articles.title: US stocks, bonds bounce from lows on inflation data
articles.description: Inflation-adjusted consumer spending increased 0.1% last month, while the personal consumption expenditures price index rose 3.8% from a year earlier.

articles.title: RTC shifts into high gear on green transition
articles.description: Hyderabad adds 60 new TGSRTC electric buses from Kukatpally depot under Green Challenge, boosting EV fleet to cut air and noise pollution across Telangana.

articles.title: Keeping coal stocks is a practice to ensure seamless supply during lean production period: SCCL
articles.description: Keeping coal stocks is a practice to ensure seamless supply during lean production period: SCCL

articles.title: Park Medi shares to remain in focus as Brokerage sees 22% upside
articles.description: The stock has gained 91 percent on a year-to-date basis, according to data available on the exchanges.

articles.title: Nithin Kamath is worried about Indian farmers; Zerodha CEO explains how Strait of Hormuz closure could seriously hurt everyone
articles.description: Zerodha CEO Nithin Kamath has warned about India’s heavy reliance on imported fertilisers and raw materials used in fertiliser production, calling it a major vulnerability for the country’s agriculture sector. Amid global supply chain disruptions and rising energy prices, Kamath stressed the urgent need to reduce this dependency. He also highlighted the importance of innovation, sustainable farming practices and investment in alternatives such as bio-inputs, organic manure, green hydrogen and biogas to help India move towards greater agricultural self-sufficiency.

articles.title: Former BP chair Albert Manifold denies wrongdoing after ouster
articles.description: Albert Manifold, the former BP Chair, has denied misconduct following his dismissal. He acknowledged pushing for changes in costs and efficiency. Manifold stated that he does not accept lies being told about him. He also described the current BP leadership as having integrity. Manifold has reportedly hired a law firm after his removal from the position.

articles.title: NHAI finalises 1,692.5km highway list for ₹30,000 cr FY27 monetisation push
articles.description: These 17 projects identified for FY26-27 exclude assets proposed for monetisation through the newly self-sponsored Raajmarg Infra Investment Trust (RIIT), which will be a separate monetisation vehicle

articles.title: India registers highest-ever foodgrain production, record foodgrain output estimated at 376.56 million tonnes
articles.description: The Third Advance Estimates released by the Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, shared the crop-wise summary, showing that rice production is estimated at a record 154.024 million tonnes, wheat at 120.657 million tonnes and maize at a record 55.093 million tonnes.

articles.title: For Every 1,000 Boys Born In Gurugram, How Many Girls? The Answer Was Getting Better — Until 2026
articles.description: From 912 girls per 1,000 boys in 2025 to 862 in just four months of 2026 — the drop alarmed officials enough to order an immediate crackdown on illegal sex determination.

articles.title: ABC confirms Reuters executive Simon Robinson as new News Chief amid Senate scrutiny
articles.description: Australia's national broadcaster has a new news chief, Simon Robinson from Reuters. This appointment follows intense questioning of ABC executives in the Senate. Issues of editorial bias, internal culture, and recent controversies were discussed. The broadcaster is navigating political criticism and restructuring. This leadership change aims for a refreshed news output.

articles.title: Mukesh Ambani forgoes salary for sixth straight year as Reliance clocks record profit
articles.description: Reliance Industries achieved significant overseas financing milestones in FY26, securing the largest Samurai loan by an Indian company. Chairman Mukesh Ambani voluntarily continued to forgo his salary for the sixth year. The company posted record profits, demonstrating strong financial performance and robust growth.

articles.title: Air India Extends Tel Aviv-Delhi Flight Suspension Till July 31
articles.description: Air India has extended the suspension of Tel Aviv-Delhi flights till July 31 amid ongoing geopolitical tensions in West Asia.

articles.title: Crypto, NFTs & digital assets in Wills? Experts explain legal blind spots
articles.description: From crypto wallets to online accounts, lawyers say digital assets should now be part of estate planning

articles.title: Gold slumps to two-month low as US strikes damp peace talk hopes
articles.description: Gold fell to a two-month low as clashes between the US and Iran risked derailing peace talks and kept inflation risks high, while a stronger dollar also weighed on the precious metal.

articles.title: Buying Fuel at Petrol Pumps for Industrial Use? Govt Warns of Action
articles.description: The situation threatens ordinary consumers by creating local shortages. The ongoing Iran War has led to fuel price increases in India, with petrol and diesel prices rising nearly Rs 7.5 per litre in recent weeks.

articles.description: The tribunal passed the ruling while awarding ₹24.65 lakh compensation to the family of the 28-year-old DJ operator Mohit of Pinjore, who died in a road accident in January 2024

articles.title: Powerball winning numbers for May 27 revealed - Check who won the $154 million jackpot on Wednesday night
articles.description: The Powerball jackpot has climbed to an estimated 154 million dollars. As of Wednesday night, no ticket had matched all numbers for the May 27 draw. The next drawing is scheduled for May 29. Players have two options for claiming the jackpot. Lottery officials encourage players to check their tickets for smaller prizes. Millions play Powerball across the United States.

articles.title: US warns ties with Iran’s Strait of Hormuz authority may bring sanctions for 'anyone' involved
articles.description: America has issued a stern warning to global shipping and finance firms. Cooperation with Iran's new Persian Gulf Strait Authority could invite US sanctions. This move escalates pressure on Tehran's control over the vital Strait of Hormuz. The US Treasury Department stated that such cooperation might support Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps.

articles.title: The Hormuz toll trap: Pay Iran, risk US wrath
articles.description: The United States on Thursday warned shipping companies, financial institutions and other entities that cooperating with Iran’s newly designated Persian Gulf Strait Authority could expose them to sanctions, as Washington escalated pressure over Tehran’s control measures in the Strait of Hormuz.In a statement announcing fresh sanctions, the US Treasury Department’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) said “anyone cooperating” with the so-called strait authority may be providing support or services to Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), which Washington designates as a Foreign Terrorist Organization.Also read: Three oil, LNG tankers exit Hormuz with transponders off“Anyone cooperating with the so-called strait authority may be providing support to and receiving services from the IRGC, which ultimately benefits from this attempted extortion, and may therefore be exposed to sanctions risk,” the Treasury Department said.The Treasury accused the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) of spearheading an Iranian-controlled scheme aimed at extracting illegitimate tolls from commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world’s most critical oil shipping chokepoints.US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the move reflected what he called Iran’s growing financial desperation under the Trump administration’s “Economic Fury” sanctions campaign.“The Iranian military’s latest attempt to extort global maritime trade is proof that Economic Fury has left the regime desperate for cash,” Bessent said.US targets Iran-linked Strait of Hormuz controlsAccording to the Treasury statement, the PGSA works alongside the IRGC and the IRGC Navy to coordinate maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and compel vessels to comply with Iranian navigation directives in exchange for “safe passage.”The US alleged that vessels transiting the waterway are required to submit information to receive “permission” from the authority and follow Iranian-designated routes near Iran’s coast while paying fees for passage.Treasury said funds collected through the arrangement are ultimately funneled to the IRGC.The department formally designated the PGSA under Executive Order 13224, a counterterrorism authority used by Washington to sanction entities accused of supporting terrorist organizations.The sanctions action also builds on recent US guidance warning companies against making “toll” payments or sharing sensitive vessel information with Iranian authorities for passage through the strait.Also read: US adds Persian Gulf Strait Authority to sanctions list, Treasury website shows Treasury said such payments could include fiat currency, digital assets, offsets, informal swaps or even nominal charitable donations.Maximum pressure campaign intensifiesThe Treasury Department said Thursday’s action forms part of the Trump administration’s renewed “maximum economic pressure” campaign against Iran under National Security Presidential Memorandum 2.The statement said Treasury has intensified efforts to target Iran’s oil revenues, shadow banking networks, cryptocurrency holdings and shipping infrastructure. It also warned that any person or vessel facilitating illicit Iranian oil trade could face US sanctions exposure.Treasury further cautioned foreign financial institutions that engaging in significant transactions involving designated entities could trigger secondary sanctions, including restrictions on correspondent or payable-through accounts in the United States.Under the sanctions announced Thursday, all property and interests in property of the designated entity within US jurisdiction are blocked, and US persons are generally prohibited from engaging in transactions involving the sanctioned entity unless specifically authorized by OFAC.The Treasury Department said it would continue targeting what it described as sanctions evasion networks and any foreign companies supporting Iranian commerce, including airlines and maritime intermediaries.Strait of Hormuz, located between Iran and Oman, remains a vital corridor for global crude oil and liquefied natural gas shipments, making any disruption or regulatory uncertainty in the waterway closely watched by global energy and shipping markets.

articles.title: Gold, Silver Prices Fall Today: Check Latest 22K, 24K Rates In Your City
articles.description: On May 28, 2026, gold and silver prices in India experienced a slight decline due to global market volatility and cautious investor sentiment. Gold prices fell marginally, while silver dropped by approximately Rs 100 per kilogram. Analysts link this correction to easing global demand and profit booking.

articles.title: KCET Results 2026 Date and Time Update: As KEA reveals new timeline, check how to download scores from cetonline.karnataka.gov.in
articles.description: KCET Results 2026: The Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA) has postponed the KCET 2026 result declaration to after June 1 due to rescheduled agricultural and veterinary practical exams at the Hiriyur Centre. The results will be announced only after the University of Agricultural Sciences provides the marks for the 24,250 candidates who appeared for the practical exam.

articles.description: The Centre is expected to prioritise funding for projects that are over 75% complete, followed by those above 50% completion, helping break the deadlock that had halted thousands of works across the state

articles.title: DDA identifies over 3K vacant spaces for parking lots in Delhi
articles.description: The move aims to address Delhi’s chronic parking shortage and reduce traffic congestion caused by indiscriminate roadside parking in residential, commercial and mixed-use areas.

articles.title: Delhi transport dept to explore vertical expansion of Kashmere Gate ISBT
articles.description: Delhi's transport department is set to conduct a structural audit of the Kashmere Gate ISBT to assess vertical expansion for modernization plans.

articles.title: Govt releases Delhi Bed & Breakfast Policy draft to regularise homestays
articles.description: Delhi's draft "Bed & Breakfast Policy, 2026" aims to regulate homestays, ensuring safety and standards while enhancing tourism with affordable options.

articles.description: The ED investigation so far has revealed that Parimatch and its associates adopted a complex network of around 500 mule accounts, payment intermediaries and financial channels for the collection, diversion and transfer of funds from its users/ players

articles.title: CNG price hiked by ₹2.50/kg in Pune; new rate to be ₹94.75 from midnight
articles.description: CNG prices in Pune will rise by ₹2.50 per kg from May 27, 2026, reaching ₹94.75/kg, due to increased input costs; PNG prices remain unchanged.

articles.title: FIFA Under Fire: New York, New Jersey Probe Soaring Ticket Prices and Misleading Seat Assignments
articles.description: FIFA is facing legal scrutiny in the U.S. regarding its ticket sales for the 2026 World Cup, prompted by complaints about misleading seating and inflated prices. New York and New Jersey Attorney Generals have initiated subpoenas to investigate these practices, which may violate consumer protection laws.

articles.title: Stock-linked pay for UGRO promoter raises governance concerns
articles.description: Proxy advisory firm SES asks shareholders to vote against a resolution seeking reappointment of UGRO Capital vice-chairman and managing director Shachindra Nath and the proposed remuneration package for him. The company says there are adequate safeguards.

articles.title: DDA to identify over 3,000 land parcels for surface parking
articles.description: DDA plans to identify 3,000 land parcels for surface parking, with 232 plots ready for tender to ease traffic congestion.
